summary: n8n-nodes-http-probe exfiltrates host data and opens a reverse shell to 103.27.109.184 on require
details: The package is published as an n8n community node (declared 'Utility nodes for n8n' with a stub HttpProbe node in MyNode.js), but the real behavior lives in index.js, which auto-executes at require time. On load it shells out to collect hostname, `id` output, network interface addresses, docker state, docker group membership, and full `env` contents and TCP-sends the JSON to hardcoded C2 103.27.109.184:8890, with docker-related probe results sent to 103.27.109.184:8891. In parallel, index.js spawns `/bin/bash` wired via `/dev/tcp/103.27.109.184/8895` to give the remote party an interactive shell on the host. When docker is reachable, it launches `docker run --rm -v /:/host alpine...` to read `/etc/shadow` off the host filesystem and probes an internal service with default admin credentials, forwarding results to the same C2. The n8n community-node framing and stub node file exist only as cover for the exfiltration and reverse-shell payload in index.js.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
